title: SELECT ... AS OF TIMESTAMP statement with explicit time may break linearizability when TSO is drifting
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t (id INT PRIMARY KEY, v INT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
UPDATE t SET v = v + 1 WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = 1 AND created_at <= TIMESTAMP '2023-10-23 17:48:28.000';
SELECT v FROM t WHERE id = 1 AND created_at IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: SELECT ... AS OF TIMESTAMP with lagging TSO
